edges.note: Both are baseline accessibility minimums.
Baseline accessibility markup/perceivability obligations.
Flush stacking relies on edge theme declaration to avoid unstyled seams.
Wave graphic count interacts with wave-edge transitions between sections.
Purple title role narrows the general text color assignments to exclude purple elsewhere.
Highlight handling reinforces the no-purple/green-in-body constraint by mandating black bold instead.
Body color role and its color restrictions apply together.
Both govern the Flip The Script campaign color system.
Opacity pairings apply to the same scene-color campaign callouts.
Email baseline overrides the general gradient-on-headline allowance, forbidding gradient text and requiring the split-headline technique instead.
Both concern the primary Dark Blue → Purple gradient.
Theme selection sets the frame; contrast pairings determine text/link colors within each themed panel.
Constrains the green accent panel step within the fixed background sequence.
Edge-to-edge transitions apply to the sequenced sections.
Locked image backgrounds are all dark, so the dark-group button rule always applies with an image.
Dark image backgrounds drive white heading/body per the text-color rule.
Image-free case is the exception where no right-side image is placed.
Narrows the golf image/teal pairing to LPGA content only.
Component reference dimensions operate within the fixed 600/375 canvas widths.
All padding/gutter values derive from the 8-pt system.
Sections rely on their own internal padding since no inter-section margin is allowed.
Both are explicit callout style treatments in the same blob.
Related callout treatments distinguished by content/scene.
Cardinality/no-substitution constraint reinforces the styling and order binding.
Placement/flow guidance applies alongside the trio's fixed style and order.
CTA section combines left-aligned text/button with right-side image.
Constrains the Heavy weight referenced by the hierarchy's ISI boxed-warning level.
Overrides the Avenir font family with Arial on email/PPT surfaces.
Boundary gaps are the resolved output of the section-owned padding tokens.
Enforces that section padding tokens are used unmodified.
Locked footer type scale and locked End Matter content both govern the same section.
Campaign headlines override the default title-case headline casing with all caps.
Campaign name in body is title-cased, an exception to the default sentence-case body copy.
Both apply within the FLIP THE SCRIPT campaign.
Prohibited targets constrain the reserved-usage set.
Both govern proper use of bold emphasis.
Same bolding/italic emphasis topic cluster.
Template-selection/subtraction constraint operates within the mandatory assembly order.
Specifies how first/last content sections meet the locked matter that brackets the stack.
Body-size matching is an inherited consequence of the locked End Matter.
Symptom phrasing supports the empathetic patient-facing voice.
Topic-matching refines the general label convention with per-section requirements.
Poll response buttons are a specific allowed CTA case under the label convention.
Both govern DTP CTA label selection.
